page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-4-lssd is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the c4a server family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory, which translates to a high memory density of 8.0 GB per core. The instance is configured with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses and contains no GPU accelerators. Although the basic description notes the presence of one local SSD, the structured local storage size is listed as None.

With a dedicated CPU allocation, this server provides predictable compute performance without resource contention from neighboring tenants. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance as a memory-optimized resource configuration. This high memory density relative to the processing core count offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require substantial memory capacity but do not demand massive parallel processing power, allowing users to avoid paying for unneeded CPU cores just to secure sufficient RAM.

The hardware profile of this server makes it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that operate within a 4-vCPU envelope. Appropriate use cases include small to medium-sized databases, caching layers, in-memory analytics, and enterprise applications with moderate CPU requirements but high memory footprints.

This allocation provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it a memory-optimized option. The server does not include GPU accelerators and has no complimentary public IPv4 addresses. While the basic description indicates the inclusion of one local SSD, the structured local storage size is listed as None. With dedicated CPU resources, the instance delivers predictable performance. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring significant memory capacity without the need to scale up CPU cores. This server is well-suited for database hosting, caching systems, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
